title: Measure and tame cold starts
description: Quantify cold-start latency, then reduce it by shrinking the package, reusing connections, and using provisioned concurrency only where it matters.
body: Use when latency-sensitive functions show tail spikes. Do not guess; and do not slap provisioned concurrency on everything to mask the issue. Actions: measure p99 init duration, trim dependencies and package size, move heavy init out of the request path (initialize clients in module scope so warm instances reuse them), pick a faster runtime if needed, and apply provisioned concurrency only to latency-critical paths. Validation signals include measured cold vs warm p99 and a reduced init duration after changes. Risk boundary: provisioned concurrency costs money continuously; escalate before enabling it broadly, and confirm the latency requirement actually justifies it.
